extraction.one_line_summary: KOOIJ Olav won from chaos after SWIFT Ben's 700m attack was neutralized by teammate AFFINI Edoardo inside final roundabouts
tactical_patterns: AFFINI Edoardo's dual role neutralizing late attacks then delivering sprinter through technical finish maximizes leadout value in chaos
PEDERSEN Mads launching sprint too early in technical finale suggests misjudgment when roundabouts compress final 300m positioning
SWIFT Ben's 700m attack exploiting technical finish demonstrates veteran opportunism can bypass pure sprint power gaps
Intermediate sprint at sub-50km creates 4-second GC swings showing vulnerability of defending yellow when bonus seconds accessible

rider_signals.KOOIJ Olav: positive: at 21, winning through technical chaos after previous day disappointment shows mental resilience and versatility beyond pure speed
rider_signals.AFFINI Edoardo: positive: successfully executing both chase duty and leadout through dangerous finale reveals elite positional control under pressure
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: negative: first failure to convert when attempting intermediate sprint this week suggests declining edge or tactical predictability
rider_signals.GAUDU David: positive: actively contesting intermediate sprint for 4-second GC gains shows willingness to race aggressively despite 6-second deficit
rider_signals.SWIFT Ben: neutral: 700m attack nearly successful but shows aging legs need chaos/surprise rather than sustained power
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: negative: launching sprint too early in technical finish indicates poor reading of compressed finale dynamics
